content_markdown:
A liquid is evaporating, causing the liquid to cool.

The temperature of the liquid decreases because

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram present in the image.

options.label: A
options.text: the number of liquid molecules is decreasing.

options.label: B
options.text: the mean kinetic energy of the liquid molecules is decreasing.

options.label: C
options.text: the pressure above the liquid surface is increasing.

options.label: D
options.text: the rate of evaporation is increasing.

vibe_explanation: Evaporation occurs when the most energetic molecules escape from the surface of a liquid. Since temperature is a measure of the average (mean) kinetic energy of the molecules remaining in the liquid, the departure of these high-energy molecules results in a lower average kinetic energy for the remaining liquid, thus decreasing its temperature.
